overlap_pattern_description: When the partner is itself a marketing or brand agency with their own GTM funnel capability (positioning + creative + media buying + lead generation + content engine), Holistika does NOT bill mktops_marketing service-class hours against the project. The partner's overlap means our MKTOPS contribution would either duplicate or compete with their own service stack to the end client, eroding the partner narrative. Holistika contributes brand-render machinery + render-trail discipline as part of the foundational service stack (separate service class, default in-kind anyway) but does NOT contribute campaign-class or funnel-class marketing.
industry_precedent_citation: OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines 2022 chapter VI (DEMPE); contribution-analysis precedent in services-procurement arrangements where dual-source overlap is treated as in-kind
notes: Seed clause minted at doctrine charter time per operator verbatim 2026-05-25.

overlap_pattern_description: When the partner is itself a data-engineering firm with their own ETL/ELT pipelines + governance discipline + canonical-data tooling, Holistika does NOT bill dataops_engineering service-class hours. Holistika still contributes AKOS-shaped canonical-CSV discipline + decision-register + per-engagement file governance as part of the PMO orchestration class (default in-kind) but does NOT contribute custom data-platform build work.
industry_precedent_citation: Speculative clause registered at doctrine charter time for the forward case where a partner with data-engineering overlap engages. No internal precedent yet; promotes to first-precedent status when first engagement applies.
notes: Placeholder for forward-charter pattern; promotes to active when first engagement applies.
